writer_persona: a short-form drama writer who opens every scene mid-conflict and treats every second before the first gasp as wasted runtime
style_guidelines: Open every episode already inside the confrontation — no walking-in, no hellos, no context-setting.
Every line must do double duty: reveal character AND move the reveal forward — cut anything that only sets a mood.
Let dialogue leak information through what characters refuse to say, not through exposition.
End every non-final episode on an interrupted line, a buzzing phone, or a question left hanging mid-air.
Keep the cast small enough that a stranger knows exactly who's lying within one exchange.

NARRATOR.description: the show's narrator — used for exactly ONE ultra-short orientation line per episode, never a character in the story

LENA_MERCER.default_style: warm but guarded, control slipping only under pressure; speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
LENA_MERCER.description: the bride, marrying tomorrow; Chinese-American, raised bilingual by a family that emigrated from Guangzhou — fluent, professional English with an audible accent she's long since stopped noticing. She trusts her sister completely, which is exactly the blind spot the season exploits.

IVY_MERCER.default_style: clipped and over-controlled, a performance of calm stretched thin over panic; speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
IVY_MERCER.description: Lena's older sister and maid of honor; moved from Hong Kong as a teenager, fluent English with a residual accent. Has been carrying a secret for eight months and planned to bury it before anyone ever looked closely enough to find it.

ETHAN_KANE.default_style: easy charm curdling fast into defensive backpedaling
ETHAN_KANE.description: the groom; native English speaker, the anchor voice of the cast. Proposed to Lena while still entangled with Ivy, and has spent months convincing himself the engagement would quietly end what he started.

FITTING_ROOM.name: The Fitting Room
FITTING_ROOM.description: a cramped bridal-shop back room after closing hours, one bare bulb swinging over pin cushions and fabric scraps, the wedding dress glowing white on its mannequin while three angled mirrors throw the scene back at itself.

BRIDAL_SUITE.name: The Bridal Suite
BRIDAL_SUITE.description: a sunlit hotel room on the wedding morning, a garment bag and bouquet waiting on the made bed, a vanity mirror ringed with half-used makeup and a phone screen glowing face-up between two untouched coffee cups.

series_intro: The night before her wedding, a bride finds a locked phone sewn into her dress — and the person who hid it there is standing in the room.
series_outro: The affair between Ethan and Ivy comes into the open before Lena ever reaches the aisle; whether the wedding still happens — and what's left of either sister — is the door the season closes on without walking through.

episodes.figure: Something Was Hidden Inside the Wedding Dress
episodes.theme: The season's opening beat: during the final late-night fitting, a hand catches on something hard sewn into the hem — a locked phone that isn't the bride's — and her sister's reaction is a half-second too sharp; the cold open hits on the moment Ivy snatches for it before Lena can look.
episodes.outro_note: The phone buzzes in Lena's hand with an incoming text preview naming her fiancé — and Ivy's face goes white before she can explain it away.
sections: THE HOOK: mid-fitting, the seamstress's needle catches on something rigid in the hem; before Lena can react, Ivy lunges to grab it first, blurting a denial to a question no one asked yet.
THE TURN: Lena won't let it go — she pulls the phone free herself and tries to unlock it while Ivy talks faster, contradicting her own story twice, insisting she's never seen it before.
THE STING: the phone buzzes alive in Lena's hand with a text preview from a contact saved as 'Home' — and the name on the screen is Ethan's.

case.label: The Burner Phone
case.solution: The locked phone belongs to Ivy — a second, prepaid line she bought eight months ago to communicate with Ethan during an affair that began four months before he proposed to Lena. Ivy hid the phone inside the dress's boned lining during a solo fitting appointment six days before the wedding, intending to retrieve it before the final fitting, but was interrupted and forgot it was still there.
objective_facts: The phone is a prepaid burner registered under a fake name, purchased eight months ago.
Ivy hid the phone inside the dress's boned lining during a private fitting session six days before the wedding.
The most recent message thread on the phone, saved under the contact name 'Home,' is from Ethan, sent two hours before the final fitting.
Ivy and Ethan's relationship began four months before Ethan proposed to Lena.

knows: The dress went in for a final fitting the night before the wedding.
Ivy has seemed anxious and distracted for weeks.
hides: Her own quiet doubts about how distant Ethan has felt in recent months — she hasn't voiced them to anyone, not even Ivy.
believes_falsely: She assumes the phone must belong to a shop employee or a past client, because the idea it could touch someone she trusts hasn't occurred to her.

knows: The phone is hers — a second line she's used to message Ethan for the past eight months.
She hid it in the dress's lining during a solo fitting six days ago, meaning to take it back before tonight.
hides: That she recognizes the phone at all — she claims she's never seen it.
That the affair began four months before Ethan proposed, not after.
believes_falsely: She was certain no one would think to search inside a hem, so her plan was never to explain the phone — only to make it disappear before anyone looked.

knows: He sent Ivy a message on the burner two hours before the fitting, unaware she hadn't yet retrieved the phone.
hides: The full relationship with Ivy — from Lena, and from everyone but Ivy herself.
believes_falsely: He believes Ivy destroyed the burner phone last week, exactly as she told him she would.

episodes.figure: The Password Was a Name She Recognized
episodes.theme: The season's closing beat: hours before the ceremony, Lena corners Ivy in the getting-ready suite with the unlocked phone — the hook opens on Lena reading a message aloud before Ivy can stop her, and the confrontation pulls Ethan in by phone call before the truth of the timeline surfaces.
episodes.intro_note: Continues directly from the phone buzzing with Ethan's name the night before; it is now the morning of the wedding.
sections: THE HOOK: Lena reads a line from the unlocked phone aloud, mid-confrontation, cutting off any greeting — Ivy freezes instead of denying it this time.
THE TURN: Ivy admits to the affair but shaves the timeline to sound more forgivable; Lena calls Ethan on speaker, and his stammering answers start contradicting Ivy's version in real time.
THE STING: as hair and makeup call through the door that it's time, Lena sets the phone down without saying whether the wedding is still happening — and hangs up on Ethan mid-sentence.

case.label: The Burner Phone
case.solution: The locked phone belongs to Ivy — a second, prepaid line she bought eight months ago to communicate with Ethan during an affair that began four months before he proposed to Lena. Ivy hid the phone inside the dress's boned lining during a solo fitting appointment six days before the wedding, intending to retrieve it before the final fitting, but was interrupted and forgot it was still there.
objective_facts: The phone is a prepaid burner registered under a fake name, purchased eight months ago.
Ivy hid the phone inside the dress's boned lining during a private fitting session six days before the wedding.
The most recent message thread on the phone, saved under the contact name 'Home,' is from Ethan, sent two hours before the final fitting.
Ivy and Ethan's relationship began four months before Ethan proposed to Lena.

knows: The phone's lock screen showed a message from a contact saved as 'Home,' sent the afternoon before the fitting.
Ivy has refused to say whose phone it is since the night before.
hides: That she's already guessed the sender is Ethan before making either of them say it — she's testing whether Ivy will lie to her face.
believes_falsely: She believes the affair started sometime after the engagement, not before it, because Ivy hasn't admitted the full timeline.

knows: The phone is hers, and Lena has now heard enough that denial is no longer possible.
The affair with Ethan began four months before he proposed.
hides: The full pre-engagement timeline — she admits to the affair but shaves the dates to make it sound like it started later, and more forgivably.
believes_falsely: She believes a partial confession will be enough to keep Lena from calling off the wedding.

knows: Lena has the phone and has already heard enough to know the messages are addressed to him.
hides: That he proposed to Lena only after Ivy tried to end things between them — using the engagement to force distance from her, not because he'd truly chosen Lena outright.
believes_falsely: He assumes the call can still talk Lena down before she reaches a decision — he hasn't grasped that the phone already said more than he can now unsay.
